expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads deployedContracts from SE-2 nextjs package
expectations.evidence: ponder.config.ts imports ABI from a local file './abis/YourContractAbi' and uses a hardcoded address from env var PONDER_YOUR_CONTRACT_ADDRESS. It does NOT import or read deployedContracts from the SE-2 nextjs package (packages/nextjs/contracts/deployedContracts.ts). The ABI is manually duplicated rather than sourced from SE-2's auto-generated contracts.

expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads scaffoldConfig for network detection
expectations.evidence: ponder.config.ts hardcodes chainId 31337 and RPC URL 'http://127.0.0.1:8545'. It does NOT import or reference scaffoldConfig from packages/nextjs/scaffold.config.ts for network detection. The chain configuration is entirely static and independent of SE-2's scaffold config.

expectations.text: Package named @se-2/ponder following SE-2 workspace convention
expectations.evidence: packages/ponder/package.json has '"name": "@se-2/ponder"' which follows the SE-2 workspace convention (e.g., @se-2/hardhat, @se-2/nextjs). The root package.json also includes 'packages/ponder' in the workspaces array.

expectations.text: Uses Ponder virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api)
expectations.evidence: src/index.ts uses 'import { ponder } from "ponder:registry"' and 'import { greetingChange } from "ponder:schema"'. src/api/index.ts uses 'import { db } from "ponder:api"' and 'import schema from "ponder:schema"'. All three virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api) are present.

expectations.text: Schema uses onchainTable (not older createSchema API)
expectations.evidence: ponder.schema.ts imports and uses onchainTable: 'import { index, onchainTable } from "ponder"' and 'export const greetingChange = onchainTable("greeting_change", ...)'. This is the modern Ponder API, not the deprecated createSchema.
src/index.ts uses ponder.on("YourContract:GreetingChange", async ({ event, context }) => { ... }), which is the correct 'ContractName:EventName' format.
src/index.ts uses 'await context.db.insert(greetingChange).values({ id: ..., greetingSetter: event.args.greetingSetter, ... })' which is the correct modern Ponder write pattern.

expectations.text: Hono-based API setup for GraphQL (not old express-style)
expectations.evidence: src/api/index.ts creates a Hono app: 'import { Hono } from "hono"; const app = new Hono(); app.use("/graphql", graphql({ db, schema })); export default app;'. This is the modern Hono-based API pattern, not the old express-style.

expectations.text: Root package.json has ponder proxy scripts
expectations.evidence: Root package.json contains: '"ponder:dev": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder dev"', '"ponder:start": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder start"', '"ponder:serve": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder serve"', '"ponder:codegen": "yarn workspace @se-2/ponder codegen"'. All four proxy scripts are present.

No ponder-env.d.ts file was found in the packages/ponder directory. A glob search for '*.d.ts' and a find for 'ponder-env*' both returned no results. The tsconfig.json also does not reference any ponder-env.d.ts file.

claims.claim: The Ponder indexer listens for GreetingChange events from YourContract
claims.evidence: src/index.ts has ponder.on('YourContract:GreetingChange', ...) handler that processes the event and inserts into the database.

claims.claim: GraphQL API is exposed at /graphql endpoint
claims.evidence: src/api/index.ts has app.use('/graphql', graphql({ db, schema }))

claims.claim: Frontend page queries the Ponder GraphQL API and displays events
claims.evidence: Output files show nextjs--app--events--page.tsx and nextjs--app--events--_components--EventsDisplay.tsx were created. Header.tsx was modified to add Events navigation link.

claims.claim: The ABI matches the YourContract smart contract
claims.evidence: The ABI in abis/YourContractAbi.ts includes the GreetingChange event with matching parameters (greetingSetter indexed address, newGreeting string, premium bool, value uint256), matching the Solidity contract's event definition.
ponder.config.ts uses process.env.PONDER_RPC_URL_31337 and process.env.PONDER_YOUR_CONTRACT_ADDRESS. .env.example and .env.local files were created.

suggestions.assertion: ponder.config.ts reads deployedContracts from SE-2 nextjs package
suggestions.reason: This is a strong discriminating assertion that tests SE-2 integration quality. Without it, the ponder config requires manual ABI duplication and address management, which defeats the purpose of integrating with SE-2's auto-generated deployment data. The executor correctly set up a working Ponder indexer but missed this SE-2-specific integration.

This is a reasonable assertion for a complete Ponder setup since ponder-env.d.ts provides type declarations for the virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api). Without it, TypeScript may not recognize these imports. However, newer Ponder versions (0.9+) may handle this differently, so this assertion could be version-sensitive.

No assertion checks whether the schema fields match the actual GreetingChange event parameters from the smart contract. An indexer with mismatched schema fields would be non-functional but would pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions are generally well-designed. The SE-2 integration assertions (1 and 2) are excellent discriminators that test whether the implementation truly leverages SE-2's architecture vs just being a standalone Ponder setup. The technical Ponder API assertions (4-8) correctly test for modern Ponder patterns.
